pubmed.title: Current and Future Development in Lung Cancer Diagnosis.
Lung Cancer 2020: Epidemiology, Etiology, and Prevention.
Recent advances in the management of lung cancer.

open_targets.name: lung cancer
open_targets.description: A malignant neoplasm involving the lung.

open_targets.name: lung carcinoma
open_targets.description: A carcinoma that arises from epithelial cells of the lung

open_targets.name: EGFR-related lung cancer
open_targets.description: Lung cancer associated with a mutation in the EGFR gene. Patients with lung cancers with EGFR mutations tend to have minimal to no smoking history.

open_targets.name: family history of lung cancer
open_targets.description: A reported family history of lung cancer in one or more family members.

hypotheses.title: Dietary sulforaphane supplementation enhances EGFR‑targeted therapy response in EGFR‑mutant NSCLC
hypotheses.mechanism: Sulforaphane activates Nrf2, lowering oxidative‑stress–driven resistance and increasing sensitivity of EGFR‑mutant tumor cells to tyrosine kinase inhibitors
hypotheses.rationale: EGFR‑mutant lung cancers have minimal smoking history and often display elevated oxidative stress; cruciferous vegetables are rich in sulforaphane which modulates Nrf2 pathways
hypotheses.next_test: Phase II trial of oral sulforaphane adjunct to osimertinib in EGFR‑mutant NSCLC patients, with progression‑free survival as endpoint

hypotheses.title: Vitamin D3 modulates tumor‑associated macrophage phenotype in KRAS‑driven lung adenocarcinoma
hypotheses.mechanism: Active vitamin D suppresses IL‑6 production and promotes M2 polarization of macrophages, potentially reducing immunosuppressive signaling
hypotheses.rationale: KRAS‑mutant tumors often create a pro‑inflammatory microenvironment; vitamin D receptors are present on immune cells and have been implicated in cancer immunotherapy outcomes
hypotheses.next_test: Ex vivo study of vitamin D3‑treated macrophages co‑cultured with KRAS‑mutant lung cancer cell lines, measuring IL‑6 and PD‑L1 expression

hypotheses.title: Intermittent fasting attenuates IGF‑1 signaling and improves response to chemotherapy in TP53‑mutant NSCLC
hypotheses.mechanism: Fasting lowers circulating IGF‑1 and insulin levels, dampening PI3K‑AKT pathway activation and enhancing DNA damage repair mechanisms
hypotheses.rationale: TP53 mutations correlate with aggressive disease; IGF‑1 signaling is a driver of proliferation in many NSCLC subtypes; dietary fasting is a non‑pharmacologic lever to modulate this axis
hypotheses.next_test: Pre‑clinical mouse xenograft study of cyclical fasting combined with cisplatin, assessing tumor growth and apoptosis markers

hypotheses.title: Omega‑3 fatty acids reshape the lipid microenvironment of EGFR‑mutant lung tumors, augmenting TKI efficacy
hypotheses.mechanism: EPA/DHA incorporation alters membrane lipid rafts, reducing EGFR clustering and restoring susceptibility to TKIs
hypotheses.rationale: Lipid metabolism is a known feature of EGFR‑mutant cancers; dietary omega‑3 influences membrane composition and inflammatory lipid mediators
hypotheses.next_test: Phase I trial of EPA/DHA supplementation alongside erlotinib in EGFR‑mutant NSCLC patients, measuring serum lipid profiles and tumor response rates

hypotheses.title: Coffee polyphenols enhance DNA repair capacity in NSCLC cells exposed to DNA‑damaging agents
hypotheses.mechanism: Chlorogenic acid and related polyphenols activate ATM/ATR pathways, increasing repair of double‑strand breaks
hypotheses.rationale: Improved DNA repair can mitigate resistance to platinum‑based chemotherapy; coffee is a major dietary source of these polyphenols
hypotheses.next_test: In vitro dose‑response experiment with caffeine metabolites and cisplatin in NSCLC cell lines, measuring γ‑H2AX foci persistence

hypotheses.title: High‑altitude hypoxia preconditioning sensitizes KRAS‑mutant tumors to MEK inhibition
hypotheses.mechanism: Transient hypoxia up‑regulates DUSP6, which feedback‑inhibits MAPK signaling and primes cells for MEK blockade
hypotheses.rationale: KRAS‑driven NSCLC often shows adaptive MAPK rewiring; hypoxia is a common feature of lung tumors and can be experimentally manipulated via environmental manipulation
hypotheses.next_test: Animal study exposing KRAS‑mutant xenografts to controlled low‑oxygen environment before MEK inhibitor treatment, assessing tumor volume and phospho‑ERK levels

hypotheses.title: Lactobacillus reuteri supplementation reduces systemic IL‑6 levels and improves immunotherapy outcomes in NSCLC
hypotheses.mechanism: The bacterium produces reutericin, which attenuates NF‑κB activation and lowers circulating IL‑6, potentially restoring responsiveness to PD‑1 blockade
hypotheses.rationale: Elevated IL‑6 is linked to immunosuppressive phenotypes in lung cancer; gut‑derived microbes can modulate cytokine production; specific strains have shown anti‑inflammatory effects in human trials
hypotheses.next_test: Phase I trial of Lactobacillus reuteri probiotic adjunct to pembrolizumab in advanced NSCLC, measuring serum IL‑6 and objective response rate

hypotheses.title: Melatonin chronotherapy optimizes timing of EGFR‑TKI administration to maximize efficacy
hypotheses.mechanism: Endogenous melatonin peaks at night and can synchronize circumscribed DNA repair cycles, enhancing drug uptake during vulnerable phases
hypotheses.rationale: Circadian regulation influences pharmacodynamics of TKIs; melatonin supplementation is safe and can be used to align dosing schedules
hypotheses.next_test: Retrospective cohort analysis of patients taking EGFR‑TKIs at night versus day, stratified by melatonin use, evaluating progression‑free survival

hypotheses.title: Polyphenol‑rich berry extracts modulate tumor‑associated macrophage infiltration via CCR2 inhibition
hypotheses.mechanism: Anthocyanins block CCR2 signaling, reducing recruitment of inflammatory macrophages that support tumor growth
hypotheses.rationale: High CCR2 expression correlates with poor prognosis in lung adenocarcinoma; dietary polyphenols have documented anti‑CCR2 activity in pre‑clinical models
hypotheses.next_test: In vivo mouse study delivering freeze‑dried berry powder to CCR2‑high lung tumor models, measuring macrophage density and tumor growth

hypotheses.title: Selenium supplementation enhances platinum drug‑induced apoptosis in p53‑wildtype lung cancers
hypotheses.mechanism: Selenoproteins increase ROS generation and impede glutathione compensation, leading to heightened oxidative damage when exposed to cisplatin
hypotheses.rationale: Selenium status varies with diet and has been linked to cancer outcomes; platinum drugs rely on ROS for cytotoxicity; boosting ROS buffering pathways can sensitize cells
hypotheses.next_test: Phase II trial of selenomethionine alongside cisplatin in p53‑wildtype NSCLC, monitoring redox markers and adverse events
